task.title: Working offline in a z/OS project
task.shortdesc: You can work offline in a z/OS project if your circumstances do not permit you to remain connected to the remote system. When you move a z/OS project to the offline state, you can select specific subprojects, data sets, and partitioned data set members to be taken offline. When you are ready to work online again, you can reconnect to the remote system and upload the resources you changed.

taskbody.prereq:
Before you can move a z/OS project to offline state, you must complete the following tasks:
Define a z/OS system and connect to the system in the Remote Systems view.
Create a z/OS project and one or more MVS subprojects in the view.
Populate the MVS subprojects with your personal data sets or partitioned data set members as well as some shared copybook data sets.
Correctly define the SYSLIBs for the remote system.
taskbody.context: The work offline and work online operations for a remote resource are deprecated.The default configuration of grants permission to move a z/OS project to offline state. If you notice that the Work Offline action is disabled for a z/OS project, then the remote system might be configured to deny permission to work offline. Consult your systems administrator if you have questions about offline permissions on a remote system.

steps.stepsection: To work offline in a z/OS project, follow these steps:

step.cmd: In the view, right-click the project to be moved offline.

step.stepresult: The Work Offline wizard opens. This wizard steps you through the process of selecting resources to be moved offline and checking dependencies to ensure that you have all the resources you must work while disconnected from the remote system.

step.cmd: On the Move Project Offline page, select the subprojects, data sets, or members to move offline.
To add the dependencies of any selected COBOL or PL/I programs to the subproject, follow these steps:

substep.stepresult: The Show Dependencies for Selected Resources page opens.

substep.info: uses the COBOL and PL/I compiler options to determine program dependencies. For information about setting compiler options for COBOL and PL/I programs, see .
substep.stepresult: The dependencies are displayed in the Dependency Files list.

substep.cmd: Select the check box next to the copy members and include files in the Dependency Files list to add these files to the subproject before the project goes offline.
substep.stepresult: The files that are selected in the dependencies list are also taken offline. If any of your programs depend on a file that cannot be found, an error message is issued and the file is skipped. To ensure that files are not skipped, run a local syntax check on your program; if the syntax check does not produce any errors, then all the dependencies can be located and no files are skipped.

step.stepresult: The selected resources are taken offline, and the project is labeled [Offline] in the view. Other project resources that were not taken offline are disabled in the z/OS Projects view. The following example shows an offline project in which USER33.RDZDB2.COBOL(RDZDB2) is offline. The remaining data sets in Subproj1 are disabled and are not accessible while the project is offline.An offline project

step.cmd: Disconnect from the remote system and work offline on downloaded resources.

taskbody.result:
When the project is taken offline, the library properties that are used to build a file are transferred to the offline subproject. In addition to the SYSLIB being transferred to reflect a local version, the Additional JCL is processed into SET statements of the Environment variables field. Translating these properties locally helps you continue development activities like syntax check, open copy member, and content assist.
The work offline feature is a convenience that you can use to edit source code, check the syntax of the code, and save local changes when a remote system connection is not available. You can perform the following actions on an offline project:
Rename data sets and partitioned data set members
Copy data sets and partitioned data set members
Delete a partitioned data set member
Edit data sets and partitioned data set members
Local syntax check
Nominate as entry point
Associate property groups with resources
Edit properties of subprojects, partitioned data sets, and partitioned data set members
Be aware, however, that the following special considerations and limitations are in effect when you work offline in a z/OS project:
Resources on the remote system are still accessible to others. You must merge your changes with any changes other users make to resources. If you choose to overwrite a remote resource when you take a project back online, any changes that others make to the resource on the remote system are lost.
For MVS, remote operations, including remote syntax check, project builds, allocation or deletion of data sets, creation or deletion of a member, move, copy and paste, rename, save as, generate JCL, debug, compress, and migration of data sets are not supported when you work offline, even if the system associated with the project is connected. For z/OS UNIX System Services, project builds, file level compilation, and assemble and debug are not supported in offline state.
New data sets cannot be created in an offline subproject.
